intended_use: BriefCase is a radiological computer aided triage and notification software indicated for use in the analysis of chest CTs (with or without contrast). The device is intended to assist hospital network
intended_use_full: BriefCase is a radiological computer aided triage and notification software indicated for use in the analysis of chest CTs (with or without contrast). The device is intended to assist hospital networks and appropriately trained medical specialists in workflow triage by flagging and communication of suspect cases of three or more acute Rib fracture (RibFx) pathologies.
target_condition: Three or more acute rib fractures on chest CT
ground_truth: Ground truthing performed by two radiologists with an additional third radiologist to resolve inconsistencies. 91 ground truth positive cases (three or more rib fractures) and 188 ground truth negative cases included in analysis.
other_metrics: Time-to-notification: mean 4.2 minutes (95% CI: 3.9-4.5); median 4.2 minutes (IQR 1.8). Standard of care time-to-exam-open: mean 89.4 minutes (95% CI: 56.0-122.7); median 66.0 minutes (IQR 50.7). Mean time difference: 85.2 minutes (95% CI: 51.8-118.6); median 63.1 minutes (IQR 50.1).
